title: tulipa humilis var. aucheriana
intro: Tulipa humilis var. aucheriana, a species of tulip commonly known as Aucher's tulip, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the lily family. Traditionally, the bulbs of Aucher's tulip have been used in herbal medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Tulipa humilis var. aucheriana are thought to contribute to various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate skin irritations. The bulbs of this plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
